sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
The fukui-reactivity task on aniline was performed using the chemkit tool.

**Fukui Reactivity Indices** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: built from user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz`; this was a single-point calculation on that geometry. SCF converged. Cation charge 1, multiplicity 2; anion charge -1, multiplicity 2. Partial charges calculated with Mulliken (PySCF) scheme.

**Results:**
The most electrophilic site (highest f+ value) is carbon atom 3 (C) with an f+ value of 2.2975867.
The most nucleophilic site (highest f- value) is nitrogen atom 1 (N) with an f- value of 0.20452359.

The fukui f+ values for each atom (N, C, C, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H, H, H, H) are: [1.6696628, -1.9009183, 2.2975867, 2.2965637, -0.28837102, -0.28784778, 1.4031744, -0.35138764, -0.35134383, -0.82107487, -0.82130626, -0.34268953, -0.75088374, -0.75116473].

The fukui f- values for each atom (N, C, C, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H, H, H, H) are: [0.20452359, -0.0037260934, 0.066571393, 0.066588805, 0.0083458973, 0.0083790984, 0.10973255, 0.08199599, 0.082013935, 0.076354671, 0.0763456, 0.087365768, 0.067758118, 0.067750673].

The fukui f0 values for each atom (N, C, C, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H, H, H, H) are: [0.93709321, -0.95232219, 1.182079, 1.1815763, -0.14001256, -0.13973434, 0.7564535, -0.13469582, -0.13466495, -0.3723601, -0.37248033, -0.12766188, -0.34156281, -0.34170703].

**Warnings:**
sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
